conversations_memory:
**Work context**

Yoyoyo is a third-year student in the "Kuģu mehānika" (Ship Mechanics) program at RTU LJA Jūrskola (group 4M129_01), with completed sea practice aboard the oil/chemical tanker M/T Elandra Maple. Yoyoyo has hands-on engine room experience including work with MAN B&W ME-series electronically controlled engines, AutoChief 600, and related maritime systems.

**Personal context**

Yoyoyo is comfortable working across multiple languages — Latvian, Russian, and English — often mixing them depending on context. Yoyoyo has a side interest in crypto trading analysis (Binance, Russian-language crypto communities) and has been developing a Discord bot project (OfficeGangBot) as a personal coding project.

**Top of mind**

Yoyoyo recently completed a significant push on OfficeGangBot — a Python/discord.py Discord bot project hosted at `https://github.com/Rub1n1337/officegangbot` — going through a full code audit, GitHub setup from scratch, and multiple rounds of AI-assisted fixes and feature development via Devin. Yoyoyo also prepared a Latvian-language maritime law presentation analyzing a watch engineer's contract expiry at sea, covering MLC 2006, SOLAS, STCW, and LSC SIA internal procedures, drawing on personal shipboard experience.

**Brief history**

*Recent months*

Yoyoyo worked on filling out the TRB (Technical Record Book) for Elandra Maple, extracting real data from ship manuals stored in Google Drive and from a personal practice report. Entries covered auxiliary machinery, steering gear, anchors, and deck machinery. Yoyoyo also completed TRB cadet duty sections referencing actual tasks: engine room rounds, tank soundings, noon counter readings, oil/chemical inventory, and lifting equipment maintenance — with output formatted to RTU LJA standards (Times New Roman 12pt, 1.5 spacing, justified). A 12-slide Latvian internship presentation outline was also developed covering the full Elandra Maple voyage (Paranaguá → India → UAE) and major maintenance works.

Yoyoyo prepared a detailed Latvian-language presentation on MAN B&W ME engine control systems, drawing on uploaded AutoChief 600 and MAN training PDFs, with specific page-level image references. Content focused on the three-level control hierarchy, hardware components (EICU, ECU, CCU, FIVA, HCU, HPS), PMI auto-tuning, safety systems, and CoCoS-EDS. Yoyoyo communicates in Russian even when presentation content is in Latvian, and prefers component-level technical precision over general descriptions.

Study sessions covered: maritime safety first aid (CPR, ĀTRI stroke method, burn/hypothermia treatment) for a kuģošanas drošība test; a 26-question maritime fire safety quiz (SOLAS, FSS/LSA Code, fire classes) with active cross-referencing against other AI tools; and a 44-question LSA quiz covering liferaft specs, pyrotechnics, and immersion suit standards.

Crypto-related work included refining PUMP/USDT trading analysis posts for Binance in colloquial Russian, with Fibonacci levels, R/R ratios, and macro BTC correlation context — explicitly styled to not read as AI-generated.

*Earlier context*

Yoyoyo engaged in extended thermodynamics exam preparation covering gas laws, polytropic processes, Carnot/Otto/Diesel/Rankine/Brayton cycles, compressor stages, and refrigeration cycles — first in Latvian, then restated in Russian upon request. A separate marine electrical engineering homework session (Kuģu elektrotehnika) covered reversing motor control circuits, brushless synchronous generator AVR systems, and motor parameter calculations. A practical stick welding (MMA) session covered electrode selection, current settings, multi-pass technique, and machine calibration variation.

*Long-term background*

Yoyoyo has a consistent pattern of academic and practical technical work spanning maritime engineering coursework, hands-on sea practice documentation, and multilingual study preparation. Google Drive MCP tool usage has recurred across multiple sessions for accessing ship manuals and company documents (LSC SIA folder IDs noted in past sessions).
